questions.q: In which present-day Indian state are the Khajuraho temples located?
questions.explanation: The Khajuraho group of monuments stands in the Chhatarpur district of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: The Khajuraho temples are most famous for which feature?
questions.explanation: Khajuraho is renowned worldwide for its richly detailed and sensuous stone sculpture.

questions.q: Which dynasty built the Khajuraho temples?
questions.explanation: The temples were built by rulers of the Chandela dynasty of central India.

questions.q: During roughly which centuries were the Khajuraho temples built?
questions.explanation: Most of the temples were constructed between roughly 950 and 1050 CE, in the 10th and 11th centuries.

questions.q: What international recognition do the Khajuraho monuments hold?
questions.explanation: The Khajuraho Group of Monuments was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1986.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of the Khajuraho structures?
questions.explanation: The Khajuraho monuments are a group of Hindu and Jain temples.

questions.q: Besides Hindu temples, which other religion's temples are also found at Khajuraho?
questions.explanation: Khajuraho includes a group of Jain temples alongside its Hindu temples.

questions.q: What is the main building material of the Khajuraho temples?
questions.explanation: The temples were built largely from fine-grained sandstone, much of it warm-toned.

questions.q: The poetic phrase often used to describe Khajuraho's artistry is best captured as which of these?
questions.explanation: Khajuraho is widely celebrated as 'poetry in stone' for its lyrical sculptural detail.

questions.q: Which is the largest and most famous temple at Khajuraho?
questions.explanation: The Kandariya Mahadeva Temple is the largest and most ornate of the Khajuraho temples.

questions.q: To which principal deity is the Kandariya Mahadeva Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: The Kandariya Mahadeva Temple is dedicated to Shiva, with a linga in its sanctum.

questions.q: The towering spire over a Khajuraho temple's sanctum is known by which term?
questions.explanation: The curvilinear spire rising above the sanctum is called the shikhara in North Indian (Nagara) architecture.

questions.q: Khajuraho temples are a classic example of which broad architectural tradition?
questions.explanation: Khajuraho exemplifies the Nagara style of North Indian temple architecture.

questions.q: The Khajuraho temples are conventionally divided into how many main geographic groups?
questions.explanation: The temples are grouped into three clusters: the Western, Eastern, and Southern groups.

questions.q: Which group of Khajuraho temples is the largest and contains the most celebrated temples?
questions.explanation: The Western group is the largest and includes Kandariya Mahadeva and Lakshmana temples.

questions.q: The Lakshmana Temple at Khajuraho is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Lakshmana Temple is dedicated to Vishnu, enshrining a Vaikuntha (multi-headed) image.

questions.q: Approximately how many temples are believed to have originally existed at Khajuraho?
questions.explanation: Historical accounts suggest around 85 temples were built, of which about 20-25 survive.

questions.q: Roughly how many temples at Khajuraho survive today?
questions.explanation: Only about 20 to 25 of the original temples remain standing today.

questions.q: The famous erotic carvings make up roughly what share of the total sculpture at Khajuraho?
questions.explanation: Erotic figures are only a small fraction; most sculptures depict gods, dancers, musicians, and daily life.

questions.q: Which Chandela ruler is traditionally credited with building the Lakshmana Temple?
questions.explanation: The Lakshmana Temple is generally attributed to the Chandela king Yashovarman, also called Lakshavarman.

questions.q: Which medieval traveller's account is an important early reference to Khajuraho?
questions.explanation: The 14th-century traveller Ibn Battuta recorded a visit to Khajuraho and its temples.

questions.q: What is the carved ceremonial entrance porch of a Khajuraho temple called?
questions.explanation: The entrance porch at the front of the temple is the ardhamandapa ('half-hall'), which leads into the larger mandapa and then the sanctum.

questions.q: What was the meaning of the word from which the name 'Khajuraho' is derived?
questions.explanation: The name is linked to 'khajur', the date palm, which gave the area its name (Khajura-vahaka).

questions.q: The inner sanctum housing the temple's main image is known by which Sanskrit term?
questions.explanation: The garbhagriha ('womb chamber') is the sacred sanctum holding the principal deity.

questions.q: British engineer T. S. Burt is credited with bringing Khajuraho to wider attention in which century?
questions.explanation: T. S. Burt drew attention to the overgrown temples in 1838, in the 19th century.

questions.q: The Chausath Yogini Temple at Khajuraho is notable for being which of the following?
options: One of the oldest surviving temples, built of granite
questions.explanation: The Chausath Yogini Temple is among the oldest at the site and is built of granite rather than sandstone.

questions.q: The Parsvanatha Temple, the largest in the Eastern group, belongs to which religious tradition?
questions.explanation: The Parsvanatha Temple is a Jain temple and the largest of the Eastern group.

questions.q: What was the approximate height of the towering shikhara of the Kandariya Mahadeva Temple?
questions.explanation: The Kandariya Mahadeva's shikhara rises about 31 metres (roughly 102 feet) above the base.

questions.q: The clustered miniature spires that rise around a Khajuraho temple's main shikhara are called what?
questions.explanation: The smaller subsidiary spires clustered around the main shikhara are known as urushringas.

questions.q: After the decline of the Chandelas, Khajuraho's remote location largely helped the temples to do what?
options: Survive widespread destruction by being forgotten in dense forest
questions.explanation: Their remoteness and jungle cover helped many temples survive, hidden until rediscovery in the 19th century.
